channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Helping 'big personality' marsupial recover after bushfires left them homeless
item.description: Artificial habitats are giving the Kangaroo Island dunnart a chance at recovery, after bushfires left them largely homeless.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kelly

media:group.media:description: Without a clear picture of the dunnart population before the fire, researchers are now able to monitor the species more closely.

item.title: Bullying, racism, homophobia met with silence in grassroots footy
item.description: The ABC has spoken to several families who say they have been failed by the AFL Integrity Unit following bullying, racism and harassment.
item.dc:creator: Marnie Vinall and Andy Burns

media:group.media:description: Dustin* loves football but alleged bullying at his local club also made him hate it.

item.title: 'His left side was missing:' How teen survived horrific shark attack
item.description: Fourteen-year-old Sam Nai survived a devastating shark attack with a desperate swim for survival, followed by 20 surgeries to reconstruct his abdomen.
item.dc:creator: Baz Ruddick

media:group.media:description: Sam Nai had weekly surgeries for four months during his recovery.

item.title: Ski resorts remain optimistic despite forecast for warmer winter
item.description: Alpine business owners and resort managers say they are not overly concerned about a warmer winter forecast, with preparations underway for the 2026 ski season ahead of the King's Birthday long weekend.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Mangelsdorf and Philippe Perez

media:group.media:description: Ski resorts are remaining optimistic despite long-term forecasts outlining a warmer winter than normal.

item.title: Australians are watching scrutiny of the Bondi attack through a keyhole
item.description: For now, Australians seeking to understand any intelligence gaps, failures, missed warnings and government failings before the Bondi massacre are expected to accept a keyhole view.
item.dc:creator: Sean Rubinsztein-Dunlop

media:group.media:description: There was little public challenge to the decisions and testimony of ASIO director-general Mike Burgess, pictured, or NSW Police and AFP counter-terrorism chiefs.

item.title: The nine books our critics couldn't put down in May
item.description: Sweet teen love, gay football stars, alien abductions and a murderous PhD student bent on revenge — this month's best new releases have it all.
item.dc:creator: Kate Evans, Nicola Heath, Declan Fry, Rosie Ofori Ward, Daniel Herborn, and Ying-Di Yin

media:group.media:description: Memoir, non-fiction and novels are among the best books released in May 2026, as chosen by ABC critics.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of John Farnham, 60s pop and where to live on the Moon
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

media:group.media:description: A John Farnham musical is coming to Sydney Theatre Company in 2026, and is then expected to tour nationally.

item.title: How women over 60 are still being made the butt of the joke in film
item.description: Films are four times more likely to feature a talking animal lead than a woman over 60. These charts explain the state of film for older women.
item.dc:creator: Tessa Flemming

media:group.media:description: Nia Vardalos and Demi Moore were two rare depictions of women over 60.

item.title: Canadian man accused of selling chemical online admits aiding suicide
item.description: In 2023, Interpol alerted Australian police that packages sold by the man, which contained a lethal substance, were sent to Australia, prompting authorities to check on the welfare of recipients.

media:group.media:description: Kenneth Law has pleaded guilty to 14 counts of aiding suicide at a court in Ontario.

item.title: Eight students arrested over deadly girls' school fire in Kenya
item.description: The fire, at the Utumishi Girls' Academy Senior School in Gilgil in west-central Kenya, broke out in the early hours of Thursday morning and also injured 79 students.

media:group.media:description: A parent reacts the morning after the fatal dormitory fire.

item.title: WHO says 223 suspected deaths, 906 cases in Ebola outbreak
item.description: The outbreak is threatening to overwhelm health authorities in the Democratic Republic of Congo, while nine cases have now been confirmed in neighbouring Uganda.

media:group.media:description: Uganda has closed its borders with the Democratic Republic of Congo due to the ongoing Ebola outbreak.

item.title: NATO slams 'reckless' Russia after drone hits Romanian apartment block
item.description: A Russian drone has struck an apartment building in NATO-member Romania, wounding two people.

media:group.media:description: Two people have been injured after a Russian drone crashed into an apartment complex in a Romanian city.

item.title: Blues rookie in doubt for Origin II after injury in Cronulla's win over Manly
item.description: New South Wales are set to be without exciting hooker Blayke Brailey after he suffered a suspected broken arm in Cronulla's 28-22 win over Manly.

media:group.media:description: Brailey left the field during the second half at Shark Park.

item.title: 'Loophole intact': Crossbenchers slam government's proposed gambling ad reforms
item.description: The partial ad ban will not prevent children from seeing gambling ads during footy or rugby games, Labor's draft legislation shows.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: Kids could be exposed to gambling ads, despite Labor's legislative changes.

item.title: Claims about high-profile married man's relationship raised in extortion case
item.description: A court has heard a man charged with extortion was "potentially gagged" by his bail terms in an effort to protect a high-profile married man.
item.dc:creator: Conor Byrne

media:group.media:description: The extortion case surrounding a high-profile Queensland man was heard in Cairns Courthouse today.

item.title: Blues complete famous win over Cats as skipper nails golden moment
item.description: Carlton's resurgence under interim coach Josh Fraser continues, with captain Patrick Cripps securing the Blues a third consecutive victory with a four-point win over Geelong at the MCG.
item.dc:creator: Dean Bilton

media:group.media:description: Cripps nailed the game-winning moment for his team.

item.title: Ex-police officer's messages did not amount to 'implied admissions' of rape, court told
item.description: A woman who says she was raped by a former South Australian police officer did not confront her alleged attacker "because she knew it wasn't true", a court has heard.
item.dc:creator: Eva Blandis

media:group.media:description: A former SA police officer has pleaded not guilty to two counts of rape and one count of attempted rape.

item.title: Qld health minister orders probe into treatment of alleged violent offender
item.description: Tim Nicholls has ordered an investigation to examine the interactions between an alleged violent offender and the health system after the death of a man in Childers.

media:group.media:description: Tim Nicholls has directed Queensland Health to investigate the circumstances surrounding the care of the alleged perpetrator of a violent crime spree in Childers.

item.title: Aboriginal families in Federal Court bid for traditional ownership recognition
item.description: Two groups of Indigenous families have claimed in the Federal Court they should be recognised as the traditional owners of the NT's Cobourg Peninsula.
item.dc:creator: Jane Bardon

media:group.media:description: Traditional ownership of the remote Cobourg Peninsula is being disputed in the Federal Court.

item.title: Socceroos boosted as young midfielder switches allegiance from Italy
item.description: The Socceroos have been boosted ahead of the FIFA World Cup, with young midfielder Cristian Volpato switching allegiance from Italy to Australia.
item.dc:creator: Tom Wildie

media:group.media:description: Cristian Volpato has switched allegiance from Italy to Australia on the eve of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Man who claimed mum stole Dreamworld tragedy compensation left feeling let down
item.description: A man who accused his mother of stealing compensation he received after the Dreamworld Thunder River Rapids ride disaster believes the legal system has let him down after a case against her has been discontinued by prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Michael Tetlow

media:group.media:description: Tom Hanson has suffered lifelong trauma after trying to help victims of the 2016 Dreamworld ride disaster.

item.title: Doubles role unlikely for Abdo as new Tennis Australia CEO
item.description: Incoming Tennis Australia CEO Andrew Abdo appears unlikely to take on the double workload of his predecessor Craig Tiley, by running both the national organisation and the Australian Open tournament.

media:group.media:description: Incoming Tennis Australia CEO Andrew Abdo.

item.title: Tony Abbott elected unopposed as Liberal Party president
item.description: Former prime minister Tony Abbott says he is ready to serve the Liberal Party in its time of 'existential crisis' upon his return to the political stage.
item.dc:creator: Ewa Staszewska

media:group.media:description: Tony Abbott says his priority is to rebuild the party.

item.title: Indonesian importer buys up big at NT's biggest bull sale
item.description: The Northern Territory's biggest bull sale has recorded one of its strongest results, despite high fuel prices and a big wet season.
item.dc:creator: James Elton

media:group.media:description: This year's bull sale at Katherine, in the Northern Territory, has seen one of its best results on record.

item.title: Executive defends appointment of 'Pink Ladies' mentee to Sydney council
item.description: A Western Sydney local government executive undermined recruitment processes to hire a woman she mentored for years and socialised with as part of a networking group dubbed the Pink Ladies.
item.dc:creator: Phoebe Pin

media:group.media:description: Angela Jones-Blayney is one of three women subject to a corruption probe into the City of Parramatta Council.
